mnemonics: ELVIS (Ethereum Layer Virtual Stack) - Ethereum is a multi-layer protocol stack, like a multi-layered sandwich, where each layer has a specific function (Execution, Consensus, etc.) to create the final 'dish' (decentralized application).
keyPoints: The stack is composed of five main layers: Execution (EVM), Consensus (Proof-of-Stake), Data Availability (Blob Transactions), Settlement (Base Layer), and Networking (DevP2P).
Smart contracts run on the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM), which is part of the Execution Layer.
The merge moved consensus from Proof-of-Work to Proof-of-Stake, separating the Execution Layer from the Consensus Layer client software.
commonMistakes: Confusing the Execution Layer (where smart contracts run) with the Consensus Layer (which orders and finalizes blocks).
Thinking the EVM is a physical machine or a single server, rather than a distributed virtual machine running on all nodes.
Believing that 'gas' is a cryptocurrency or token, rather than a unit of measurement for computational effort.
